mdRecord.dcterms:title: James C. Biddle to Gertrude G. Meredith, October 22, 1861
mdRecord.dcterms:description: First Lieutenant James C. Biddle writes to Gertrude G. Meredith from Fort Hatteras, North Carolina, discussing Federal supply ships, food, qualities and facts about his comrades, prevalence of flies, and the possibility of hunting sharks with a pistol.
mdRecord.dcterms:subject: Civil War
United States--History--Civil War, 1861-1865
mdRecord.dcterms:rights: This Item is protected by copyright and/
mdRecord.dcterms:provenance: Virginia Polytechnic Institute and State University. University Libraries
mdRecord.dcterms:creator: Biddle, James C.
